description.en: A butter-laminated Malaysian flatbread stretched by hand until nearly see-through, then coiled, pan-fried and clapped between the palms to shatter into flaky, pull-apart layers. Sold at all-hours mamak stalls alongside dhal curry.
description.de: Ein mit Butter laminiertes malaysisches Fladenbrot, fast durchsichtig gezogen, aufgerollt, gebraten und zwischen den Händen geklatscht, bis es in zupfbare Schichten zerfällt. An Mamak-Ständen rund um die Uhr mit Dhal-Curry verkauft.
description.es: Un pan plano malayo laminado con mantequilla, estirado a mano hasta quedar casi transparente, enrollado, frito en sartén y golpeado entre las palmas hasta deshacerse en capas hojaldradas. Se vende a toda hora en los puestos mamak junto con curry de dhal.
description.fr: Un pain plat malaisien feuilleté au beurre, étiré à la main jusqu'à devenir presque transparent, enroulé, poêlé puis frappé entre les paumes jusqu'à se défaire en couches croustillantes. Vendu à toute heure dans les échoppes mamak avec un curry de dhal.

origin.note: Descended from South Indian paratha traditions brought by Tamil Muslim immigrants; roti canai and Singapore's roti prata are close siblings of the same dough.

ingredients.note: for coating dough balls and laminating during stretching

yield.shape: thin stretched round or square, pan-fried, 20-25 cm

keeping.note: Best hot off the griddle, torn and fluffed by hand; keeps poorly once cool. Uncooked dough balls freeze well and cook straight from frozen, like the common frozen roti canai sold in stores.

method.title: Mix & knead
method.summary: Combine flour, egg, melted butter, condensed milk, water and salt; knead about 10 minutes, rest 10, then knead 5 more until smooth.

method.title: Butter & chill
method.summary: Divide into balls, coat each generously in butter, and refrigerate overnight in a covered, buttered container.

method.title: Stretch thin
method.summary: The next day, flatten each ball and stretch it by hand, working in more butter, until almost translucent.

method.title: Fold & coil
method.summary: Gather the thin sheet into a loose log, then coil it into a spiral and tuck the end under.

method.title: Rest
method.summary: Let the coiled dough rest about 10 minutes, then press flat.

method.title: Pan-fry
method.summary: Cook in a buttered pan over medium heat, several minutes per side, until deep golden.

method.title: Clap & serve
method.summary: Clap the hot bread between your palms to fluff and separate the layers before serving.

source.adaptation: Lamination butter quantity beyond the dough itself estimated at roughly 100 g across 10 pieces, since the source calls for 'additional butter for coating and cooking' without a fixed amount.
